events.text: Linda Harris, 49-year-old Black female, Never smoker. Lives independently. ECOG performance status 1.

Diagnosed with Clear cell renal cell carcinoma, Fuhrman grade 3 of the Right kidney. Clinical staging: T3bN0M1, AJCC Stage IV. ICD-10: C64.9. Stage IV clear cell renal cell carcinoma with lung metastases.
CT chest/abdomen/pelvis with contrast performed for baseline staging.
Pathology specimen received for initial biopsy.
Clinical visit for initial oncology consultation.
NGS report received for molecular profiling.
Underwent cytoreductive nephrectomy: Right radical nephrectomy with adrenalectomy. Estimated blood loss 400 mL. Length of stay 6 days. Uncomplicated postoperative course.
Pathology specimen received for surgical pathology.
Clinical visit for postoperative follow-up.
Initiated first-line immunotherapy-TKI combination: Pembrolizumab 200 mg q3w + Axitinib 5 mg BID. Cycle 1. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Developed grade 2 fatigue. Activity modification recommended. TSH and CBC checked - within normal limits.
Initiated first-line immunotherapy-TKI combination: Pembrolizumab 200 mg q3w + Axitinib 5 mg BID. Cycle 2. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Clinical visit for cycle 2 assessment.
Initiated first-line immunotherapy-TKI combination: Pembrolizumab 200 mg q3w + Axitinib 5 mg BID. Cycle 3. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Developed grade 2 diarrhea. Managed with loperamide 4 mg then 2 mg after each loose stool. Stool studies negative.
Initiated first-line immunotherapy-TKI combination: Pembrolizumab 200 mg q3w + Axitinib 5 mg BID. Cycle 4. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Clinical visit for cycle 4 assessment.
Developed grade 2 fatigue. Activity modification recommended. TSH and CBC checked - within normal limits.
MRI abdomen with contrast performed for restaging after first-line immunotherapy-TKI combination.
Clinical visit for results review after first-line immunotherapy-TKI combination.
Initiated mTOR inhibitor: Everolimus 10 mg daily. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Clinical visit for follow-up on mTOR inhibitor.
Clinical visit for follow-up on mTOR inhibitor.
MRI abdomen with contrast performed for restaging on mTOR inhibitor.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.
MRI abdomen with contrast performed for surveillance imaging.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.
CT chest/abdomen/pelvis with contrast performed for surveillance imaging.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.

documents.text:
CT CHEST/ABDOMEN/PELVIS WITH CONTRAST REPORT

Patient: Linda Harris
MRN: patient_575d96f60d2a
Date of Study: 2022-09-07

CLINICAL INDICATION: Clear cell renal cell carcinoma, Fuhrman grade 3 of the right kidney. baseline staging.

COMPARISON: None available.

TECHNIQUE: Multidetector CT performed from skull base to iliac crest following administration of 100 mL Omnipaque 350 IV contrast.

FINDINGS:
MEDIASTINUM: No significant lymphadenopathy. Heart size normal.

LIVER: Homogeneous attenuation. No focal lesions.

PANCREAS: Normal in size and attenuation. No pancreatic mass or ductal dilation.

KIDNEYS: Heterogeneously enhancing mass arising from the right kidney measuring 9.8 x 7.8 cm confined to the renal parenchyma.

PELVIS: No pelvic mass or lymphadenopathy. Bladder unremarkable.

BONES: No suspicious osseous lesions. Degenerative changes of the lumbar spine.

IMPRESSION:
1. Right renal mass consistent with renal cell carcinoma. Pulmonary metastases.

Electronically signed by: Jennifer Walsh, MD
Department of Radiology

PATHOLOGY REPORT

Patient: Linda Harris
MRN: patient_575d96f60d2a
Specimen ID: S2022-14674
Date of Procedure: 2022-09-09
Date of Report: 2022-09-13

SPECIMEN: Right kidney, excision/biopsy

FINAL DIAGNOSIS:
Clear cell renal cell carcinoma, Fuhrman grade 3, poorly differentiated.
Greatest tumor dimension: 7.5 cm
Margins: Positive at inked margin
Lymphovascular invasion: Present
Perineural invasion: Not identified

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY:
PAX8: Positive
CA-IX: Positive (diffuse membranous)
CD10: Positive
CK7: Negative
Vimentin: Positive

GROSS DESCRIPTION:
Received fresh labeled "Linda Harris - Right kidney" is a tan-white tissue fragment measuring 7.5 x 5.2 x 3.8 cm. The cut surface reveals a firm tan mass. Specimen entirely submitted in cassettes A1-A7.

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
Sections show a poorly differentiated neoplasm composed of sheets of atypical cells with abundant cytoplasm and high nuclear-to-cytoplasmic ratio. Mitotic figures are identified (6 per 10 HPF). Focal necrosis is present. The surrounding stroma shows minimal reactive changes.

Electronically signed by: Patricia Williams, MD
Department of Pathology

INITIAL CONSULTATION - Medical Oncology

Patient: Linda Harris
MRN: patient_575d96f60d2a
Date: 2022-09-13
Provider: Dr. Richard Okafor

CHIEF COMPLAINT: initial oncology consultation

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS:
Mr./Ms. Harris is a 49-year-old female who presents for initial evaluation of newly diagnosed clear cell renal cell carcinoma, fuhrman grade 3 of the right kidney, IV. Patient presented with symptoms prompting workup. Stage IV clear cell renal cell carcinoma with lung metastases. Pathology confirmed clear cell renal cell carcinoma, fuhrman grade 3. Molecular testing has been sent. Patient reports progressive dyspnea and cough x 6 weeks.

PAST MEDICAL HISTORY:
- hypertension
- gastroesophageal reflux disease
- chronic kidney disease stage 2

SURGICAL HISTORY:
- Cholecystectomy (age 50)

SOCIAL HISTORY:
- Former smoker (quit 5 years ago)
- Alcohol: Rare alcohol use
- Occupation: Construction worker (retired)
- Lives with spouse

FAMILY HISTORY:
- Father with colon cancer at age 70.

ALLERGIES: Latex (contact dermatitis)

MEDICATIONS:
- Lisinopril 10 mg daily
- Omeprazole 20 mg daily

REVIEW OF SYSTEMS:
Positive for: mild cough, insomnia, dizziness, unintentional weight loss. Remainder of 14-point ROS reviewed and negative.

PHYSICAL EXAMINATION:
Vitals: BP 132/88, HR 70, RR 17, Temp 98.0F, SpO2 98% on RA, Wt 90.0 kg
General: Comfortable, thin-appearing, no acute distress.
HEENT: NCAT, EOMI, PERRL. Oral mucosa moist without lesions.
Neck: No thyromegaly. No lymphadenopathy.
Lungs: Clear bilaterally. No rales or rhonchi.
CV: RRR, S1/S2 normal. No murmurs, gallops, or rubs.
Abdomen: Soft, non-tender, non-distended. No hepatosplenomegaly.
Extremities: No peripheral edema. Good pulses bilaterally.
Neuro: Alert and oriented. No focal neurological deficits.

LABORATORY RESULTS:
WBC 7.7, Hgb 13.9, Plt 152, Cr 1.15

IMAGING:
See separate imaging report.

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:
49-year-old female with IV clear cell renal cell carcinoma, fuhrman grade 3 of the right kidney. initial oncology consultation.

1. Clear cell renal cell carcinoma, Fuhrman grade 3 of the right kidney, Stage IV
   - Reviewed pathology and imaging with patient and family
   - Recommend cytoreductive nephrectomy
   - Will discuss at multidisciplinary tumor board
   - Molecular profiling results pending
2. Supportive care
   - Nutrition consultation placed
   - Social work referral for psychosocial support
   - PICC line to be placed prior to treatment
3. Follow-up in 1-2 weeks to initiate treatment

Electronically signed by: Priya Sharma, MD
Medical Oncology

NEXT GENERATION SEQUENCING REPORT

Patient: Linda Harris
MRN: patient_575d96f60d2a
Date of Report: 2022-09-23
Specimen: Right kidney (Biopsy)
Diagnosis: Clear cell renal cell carcinoma, Fuhrman grade 3
Panel: MSK-IMPACT (468 genes)

RESULTS SUMMARY:
Tumor Mutational Burden (TMB): 5.2 mutations/Mb (Low)
Microsatellite Status: MSS

CLINICALLY SIGNIFICANT GENOMIC ALTERATIONS:
  VHL p.L89P (c.266T>C) - Missense (Exon 1) [Clinically Significant]

ADDITIONAL PATHOGENIC ALTERATIONS:
  PBRM1 p.Q1298* - Nonsense (Exon 24)

VARIANTS OF UNCERTAIN SIGNIFICANCE:
  KDM5C p.A388T - VUS
  MTOR p.S2215Y - VUS

COPY NUMBER ALTERATIONS:
  No significant copy number alterations detected

FUSIONS:
  No pathogenic fusions detected.

THERAPY ASSOCIATIONS:
  - VHL mutation: Consistent with clear cell histology. HIF-pathway targeted therapies (TKIs) and immunotherapy combinations recommended.
  - BAP1 loss: Associated with more aggressive biology.

Note: This test was performed using hybrid capture-based next generation sequencing.
Sequencing depth: mean 595x coverage.
Tumor content: 22%.

Electronically signed by: Maria Santos, MD, PhD
Molecular Pathology Laboratory

PATHOLOGY REPORT

Patient: Linda Harris
MRN: patient_575d96f60d2a
Specimen ID: S2022-49520
Date of Procedure: 2022-10-10
Date of Report: 2022-10-12

SPECIMEN: Right kidney, excision/biopsy

FINAL DIAGNOSIS:
Clear cell renal cell carcinoma, Fuhrman grade 3, moderately differentiated.
Greatest tumor dimension: 4.3 cm
Margins: Negative (closest margin 0.3 cm)
Lymphovascular invasion: Not identified
Perineural invasion: Not identified

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY:
PAX8: Positive
CA-IX: Positive (diffuse membranous)
CD10: Positive
CK7: Negative
Vimentin: Positive

GROSS DESCRIPTION:
Received fresh labeled "Linda Harris - Right kidney" is a firm tan tissue fragment measuring 4.3 x 3.0 x 2.1 cm. The cut surface reveals a friable white mass. Specimen entirely submitted in cassettes A1-A5.

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
Sections show a moderately differentiated neoplasm composed of glands of atypical cells with moderate cytoplasm and irregular nuclear contours. Mitotic figures are occasionally seen (3 per 10 HPF). No necrosis is identified. The surrounding stroma shows desmoplastic reaction.

Electronically signed by: Emily Nakamura, MD
Department of Pathology

FOLLOW-UP NOTE - Urology

Provider: Dr. Amanda Foster

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS:
Mr./Ms. Harris is a 49-year-old female with IV clear cell renal cell carcinoma, fuhrman grade 3 of the right kidney who presents for postoperative follow-up. Patient reports feeling well overall. Weight is stable.

REVIEW OF SYSTEMS:
Positive for: dizziness, joint pain, mild cough. Remainder of 14-point ROS reviewed and negative.

PHYSICAL EXAMINATION:
Vitals: BP 125/67, HR 69, RR 15, Temp 97.7F, SpO2 98% on RA, Wt 77.2 kg
General: Well-nourished, alert, in no distress.
HEENT: NCAT, EOMI, PERRL. Oral mucosa moist without lesions.
Neck: No thyromegaly. No lymphadenopathy.
Lungs: Clear to auscultation bilaterally.
CV: Regular rate and rhythm, no murmurs.
Abdomen: Soft, mildly tender in RUQ. No rebound. Normoactive bowel sounds.
Extremities: No peripheral edema. Good pulses bilaterally.
Neuro: A&O x3, CN II-XII intact, no focal deficits.

LABORATORY RESULTS:
WBC 9.1, Hgb 9.6, Plt 288, Cr 0.71

IMAGING:
Most recent imaging reviewed.

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:
49-year-old female with IV clear cell renal cell carcinoma, fuhrman grade 3 of the right kidney. postoperative follow-up.

1. Clear cell renal cell carcinoma, Fuhrman grade 3 - postoperative follow-up
   - Continue current treatment regimen
   - Will check labs prior to next cycle
2. Symptom management
   - Neuropathy being monitored
3. Follow-up in 3 weeks

Electronically signed by: Priya Sharma, MD
Urology

Patient: Linda Harris

Date of Study: 2024-03-17

CLINICAL INDICATION: Clear cell renal cell carcinoma, Fuhrman grade 3 of the right kidney. surveillance imaging.

COMPARISON: Comparison made to prior study from approximately 2 months ago.

TECHNIQUE: Multidetector CT performed from skull base to iliac crest following administration of 100 mL Omnipaque 350 IV contrast.

FINDINGS:
LUNGS: Few pulmonary nodules, largest measuring 1.6 cm in the left upper lobe. Stable in size.

MEDIASTINUM: No significant lymphadenopathy. Heart size normal.

LIVER: Homogeneous attenuation. No focal lesions.

PANCREAS: Normal in size and attenuation. No pancreatic mass or ductal dilation.

KIDNEYS: Heterogeneously enhancing mass arising from the right kidney measuring 9.4 x 7.5 cm with extension into the renal vein.

PELVIS: No pelvic mass or lymphadenopathy. Bladder unremarkable.

BONES: No suspicious osseous lesions. Degenerative changes of the lumbar spine.

IMPRESSION:
1. Right renal mass consistent with renal cell carcinoma. Pulmonary metastases.
2. Overall assessment: Stable disease per RECIST 1.1.

Electronically signed by: Jennifer Walsh, MD
Department of Radiology
